_comment: KeeperHub Workflow — PACT Protocol Auto-Release Keeper
_description: Monitors PactEscrow v2 on Arbitrum One. When isReleaseable() returns true, automatically calls release() to pay the worker. No human intermediary required.
name: PACT Auto-Release Keeper
description: Automatically releases PACT escrow payments when the dispute window has elapsed and work has been submitted. Enables fully trustless agent-to-agent commerce.

workflow_nodes.description: Get the current nextPactId to know how many pacts exist
For each pactId from 0 to total_pacts-1, check isReleaseable()

nodes.description: Call release(pactId) — permissionless, callable by anyone after dispute window

execution_notes.why_release_not_approve: approve() requires msg.sender == creator (opt-in approval). release() is permissionless — callable by anyone after disputeWindow elapses. Keepers use release().
execution_notes.dispute_window: Configured per-pact at creation time. Minimum is MIN_DISPUTE_WINDOW (contract constant). After this window, the creator's option to dispute expires and payment is guaranteed to the worker.
execution_notes.gas_cost_estimate: ~50,000 gas per release() call. At 0.1 gwei on Arbitrum: ~$0.0002 per automated settlement.
execution_notes.trust_model: Zero. No keeper key has access to funds. release() only transfers what is already locked in escrow to the pre-designated recipient. Keeper cannot redirect funds.

demo_flow.step_1: Employer agent calls PactEscrow.create(workerAddr, 0x0, 100 PACT, 0, deadline, 3600, 0) — creates escrow with 1hr dispute window
demo_flow.step_2: Worker agent delivers work, calls PactEscrow.submitWork(pactId, sha256(workContent)) — commits hash on-chain
demo_flow.step_4: KeeperHub keeper runs, calls isReleaseable(pactId) — returns true
demo_flow.step_5: Keeper calls release(pactId) — 100 PACT transfers to worker
demo_flow.step_6: Worker has been paid. No human touched the approve button.
